# Timegrapher.ai

> Free browser-based timegrapher for mechanical watches. Measures rate, beat error, amplitude and BPH using microphone input, with instant AI diagnosis.

Timegrapher.ai is a single-page web application that works as a digital timegrapher for mechanical watches. There is nothing to install and no account: users allow microphone access in their browser, rest the watch near the microphone, and get live measurements within seconds.

## What it measures

- **Rate (seconds per day)**: how many seconds per day the watch gains (+) or loses (−). The COSC chronometer standard is −4 to +6 s/d; ±15 s/d is acceptable for everyday wear.
- **Beat error (milliseconds)**: the timing difference between the tick and tock of the escapement. Under 0.5 ms is excellent; over 2.5 ms can cause the watch to stop.
- **Amplitude (degrees)**: the arc through which the balance wheel swings on each beat. 220 to 310 degrees is healthy; below 200 suggests the mainspring needs winding or the movement needs a service.
- **BPH (beats per hour)**: the frequency of the movement, detected automatically. Common values: 18 000 (5 Hz), 21 600 (6 Hz), 28 800 (8 Hz), 36 000 (10 Hz).

## How it works

1. The browser captures microphone audio at 44 100 Hz via the Web Audio API.
2. A high-pass filter removes low-frequency noise while preserving tick transients.
3. A peak-follower envelope detector identifies beat events.
4. Rate, beat error, amplitude and BPH are derived with the same arithmetic used by hardware timegraphers.
5. A live paperstrip shows the historical trace of beat timing; waveform panels show the averaged tick and tock sound shapes.

## AI diagnosis

Users can tap "Diagnose my watch" to send their current readings to a secure backend. The AI diagnosis reads the numbers against the movement's specification and answers plainly: what's healthy, what's drifting, and what to fix before it becomes a repair. Users can also name their watch (for example "Seiko SKX007") to auto-fill movement specs, and ask follow-up questions about their readings.

## Privacy and requirements

- Audio is processed locally in the browser; no audio is stored or transmitted. Only AI diagnosis requests (text readings) go to the backend.
- The app works offline once loaded, except for the AI feature.
- Requires Chrome 80+, Firefox 76+, or Safari 14.5+ and a microphone.
- A clip-on contact microphone (about $15) significantly improves signal quality over a built-in laptop microphone.

## Links

- App: https://timegrapher.ai/
- Sitemap: https://timegrapher.ai/sitemap.xml
